summary: Iteration 217 — End-to-end UI validation of the new Delivery Orders module (/delivery-orders) on the preview env, logged in as CEO surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth/test123 (tenant nyla-air-water). 5 of 6 review_request acceptance scenarios PASS; the 6th (sidebar 'Delivery Orders' nav item in Sales) FAILS (link not rendered). Direct URL access works fine via ProtectedRoute. Backend integration is fully wired: create -> submit -> approve correctly auto-created a draft promo stock-out (DC-2606-0006) for the Goa distributor when delivery city = 'Margao'. Draft lifecycle works (Save as Draft -> Submit for Approval + Cancel actions available; Approve/Reject hidden in draft).

ui_bugs.issue: Sidebar nav item 'Delivery Orders' is not rendered for CEO user even though DashboardLayout.js defines it at lines 104/136/173 with CEO in the roles list. a[href='/delivery-orders'] count = 0. Most likely the moduleKey 'delivery_orders' is being filtered out by the module-visibility check (tenant module flag) — the route itself is reachable via direct URL because the page protect-wraps with moduleKey 'delivery_orders' and that succeeds, so this is specifically a sidebar filter mismatch. Users can't discover the feature from the Sales sidebar.

issues: Radix warning in console: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle for the component to be accessible for screen reader users.' Fires while the detail dialog is in its initial loading branch (when order is null), because the <DialogHeader>/<DialogTitle> is rendered only AFTER the order loads. Wrap the spinner branch in a VisuallyHidden DialogTitle or always render the title.

action_items: [HIGH] Make 'Delivery Orders' visible in the Sales sidebar for CEO. Either enable the 'delivery_orders' module flag for the tenant (if the sidebar uses moduleKey filtering against tenant settings) or whitelist it explicitly. Currently CEO sees Home/Dashboard/Leads/Pipeline/Accounts/Account GOP Metrics/Invoices/Customer Returns/Design Requests/Print Requests/Sales Portal but NOT Delivery Orders.
[LOW] Fix a11y warning in DetailDialog: always render a DialogTitle (use VisuallyHidden for the loading branch) — currently logs the Radix warning until order data is fetched.
critical_code_review_comments: DeliveryOrders.js:384-386 — when loading, the dialog renders only a spinner with no DialogTitle. Radix logs an a11y error. Add <VisuallyHidden><DialogTitle>Loading order…</DialogTitle></VisuallyHidden> inside the loading branch.

DeliveryOrders.js:62 — recipient list parsing relies on heuristic key names (data.contacts || data.leads || data.accounts || data.users). If a future endpoint changes the wrapper key (e.g. data.items), the search silently returns []. Consider standardising to a single 'data' key or accept a normaliser fn per recipient type.

DeliveryOrders.js:208-211 — on Save & Submit, the second POST (/transition submit) is fired without try/catch granularity: if the order is created but the submit POST fails, the user sees a generic error toast while the order is silently left in Draft. Surface a clearer message ('Order DO-… created but submit-for-approval failed — open the order to submit again').

DashboardLayout.js (104,136,173) — three separate copies of the same Delivery Orders nav entry across Sales/Production/Distribution modules. If the moduleKey check is what's hiding it, fix once at the moduleKey-resolution layer rather than per-module overrides. Also worth verifying the tenant moduleconfig actually exposes 'delivery_orders' — currently the Sales sidebar omits it for CEO.

success_rate.frontend: 83% (5/6 acceptance scenarios) — only sidebar visibility fails

seed_data_creation: Two preview-data orders created during this run (explicitly authorized in the review_request): DO-2026-0001 (recipient Contact 'Testing kajslfsa', Margao, Promotion reason, 1 line Nyla – 600 ml / Silver × Crate-12 × qty 2 = ₹240) created via Create & Submit -> Pending Approval -> Approved by CEO. Approval auto-created draft promo stock-out DC-2606-0006 for the Goa-covering distributor. DO-2026-0002 (same recipient, same SKU × qty 1 = ₹120) saved as Draft (still Draft; Submit for Approval + Cancel actions available).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Delivery Orders data-testids in /app/frontend/src/pages/DeliveryOrders.js are correctly wired and were used live: delivery-orders-page, do-new-btn, do-state-filter, do-search, do-row-{order_number}, do-create-dialog, do-recipient-type-{lead|account|contact|employee}, do-recipient-search, do-recipient-option-{id}, do-recipient-selected, do-requested-date, do-reason, do-addr-city, do-add-item, do-item-sku-{i}, do-item-pkg-{i}, do-item-qty-{i}, do-item-price-{i}, do-total, do-save-draft, do-save-submit, do-detail-dialog, do-action-approve / do-action-reject / do-action-cancel / do-action-submit, do-fulfillment. Backend endpoints used: GET /api/delivery-orders, POST /api/delivery-orders, GET /api/delivery-orders/{id}, GET /api/delivery-orders/{id}/available-transitions, POST /api/delivery-orders/{id}/transition. Distributor that picked up DO-2026-0001 approval was the Goa distributor (city Margao routing worked). Promo reason 'Promotion' available in /api/admin/promo-reasons. SKUs that DO have packaging_config.promo_stock_out (or stock_out) usable: 'Nyla – 600 ml / Silver' (and 5 more Nyla variants). Open issue for next agent: Sidebar 'Delivery Orders' link is missing for CEO under Sales module — verify whether tenant moduleconfig exposes 'delivery_orders' and whether ProtectedRoute moduleKey matches the same flag used by the sidebar filter.
rca of the issue: Sidebar misses 'Delivery Orders': DashboardLayout.js declares the item at lines 104 (Sales), 136 (Production), 173 (Distribution) with moduleKey='delivery_orders' and CEO in the role whitelist. CEO IS in roles, the route works via direct URL navigation (so ProtectedRoute moduleKey check resolves true), but the sidebar still renders 0 occurrences of a[href='/delivery-orders']. Hypothesis: the sidebar item is filtered by a different criterion than ProtectedRoute (e.g. tenant.industry_features or industry_config in TenantConfig — see console logs 'Industry Config Loaded: {industry_type: water_brand, industry_features: Array(6), industry_config: Object}'). If 'delivery_orders' is not in industry_features for this tenant, the sidebar hides it while the direct route still allows access. Mitigation: either add 'delivery_orders' to the tenant industry_features list, or align the sidebar visibility check with ProtectedRoute (use the same moduleKey resolver). Reproduction: login as CEO -> sidebar under Sales -> scroll through items; 'Delivery Orders' never appears. Direct URL /delivery-orders renders the full page correctly.
